__comment: STAGING = a TESTNET tier (behaves like Development) running on EC2 against the TRON Nile testnet with a THROWAWAY signer. It is NOT production. This file holds ONLY non-secret, testnet-generic config. Every secret and per-box value — Db/Redis/Mongo connection strings, the Nile API key, the throwaway signing key + hot wallet, the Merchant peppers/signing keys, and the dev-seed merchant credentials — MUST be set on the EC2 box via environment variables or the git-ignored appsettings.Local.json, NEVER here (§10). See docs/ec2-staging.md. Changing ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT to Production disables the throwaway signer + all dev seeding automatically (fail-safe) and requires a KMS-backed signer.

Withdrawal.__comment_policies: TESTNET tuning: low confirmation depth so a withdrawal confirms in seconds on Nile (~3s/block). The other policy fields (Min/Max/Fee/ApprovalThreshold) come from appsettings.json.

Deposit.__comment: TESTNET tuning: low confirmation depth so a real Nile deposit credits quickly.

Blockchain.__comment: TESTNET override: USDT points at the Nile test-USDT contract (public, not a secret). Keep the canonical AssetId stable so the edge, scanner, and ledger agree.

Merchant.__comment: Version pointers only. The pepper (ApiCredentials:Peppers:<v>) and HMAC key (SigningSecrets:Keys:<v>) are SECRETS — set them in EC2 env/Local. The module refuses to start without a pepper for CurrentHashVersion.

DevSeed.__comment: A test merchant so signed /api/v1 calls work for staging testing. ApiKey/ApiSecret/SigningSecret are secrets — set them in EC2 env/Local, never commit. Set Enabled=false to require a real provisioned merchant instead.
DevSeed.Name: Staging Test Merchant
